Title: No Page Navigation
What's The Problem?: Right now, if there are 500 applications, the system only loads the first 100. The admin has no way to see applications 101-500.
What Happens To Users/Business?: When you grow past 100 customers, admin can't see new ones. You can't manage your own business.
How To Fix It: Add pagination: Show 20 results per page, add 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons. Like turning pages in a book.
What Gets Better After Fix: Admin can see all 500+ customers. They can navigate between pages. Works at any scale.

Title: Notifications Block Everything
What's The Problem?: When admin changes application status, the system sends email/WhatsApp BUT waits for it to finish before responding. If sending takes 5 seconds, admin waits 5 seconds.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Admin clicks a button, nothing happens for 5-8 seconds. They think it's broken. They click again. Performance feels terrible.
How To Fix It: Send emails/WhatsApp in the background (background job queue). Respond to admin instantly (in 100ms). Send emails after.
What Gets Better After Fix: Admin gets instant response. No waiting. Emails still go, just don't block the user. Much faster.

Title: Too Many Database Queries
What's The Problem?: When you update 1 application status, the system runs 4 separate queries. So updating 1000 applications means 4000 queries!
What Happens To Users/Business?: Database connection pool gets exhausted. System becomes slow. At scale (when you grow), everything breaks.
How To Fix It: Use transactions and batch queries. Update 1000 records with 1 query instead of 4000. Like doing assembly line vs doing things one by one.
What Gets Better After Fix: Bulk updates are 50% faster. Database handles more concurrent users. System scales better.

Title: Search is Slow
What's The Problem?: When searching for bank policies, the system runs 4 queries and searches every character of text. Should be 1 query and blazing fast.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Search takes 150ms when it should take 20ms. Users type in search and wait. Bad experience. Users leave.
How To Fix It: Use a single query with better logic (CTE with relevance scoring). Add caching so popular searches are instant.
What Gets Better After Fix: Search becomes 7.5x faster (150ms to 20ms). Users see results instantly. Better experience.

Title: Unused AI Vector Search
What's The Problem?: Loaded AI embeddings (special search data) for bank policies but never used. Semantic search not implemented. Feature doesn't exist.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Wasted code. No smart recommendations. AI capability unused. Poor search.
How To Fix It: Populate vectors when banks updated. Create /search-similar endpoint. Show users similar banks based on AI meaning.
What Gets Better After Fix: Smart search works. Users find relevant banks. AI capability realized.

Title: No Data Retention Policy
What's The Problem?: Data accumulates forever. Old users, old applications, old records all stay indefinitely. Database grows huge.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Database bloats. Speed slows. Compliance regulations violated (GDPR: delete old data). Audit fails.
How To Fix It: Define policy: Delete data after 2 years. Add scheduled job to clean old records. Legal compliance.
What Gets Better After Fix: Database stays lean. Compliance happy. No violations. Legal risk gone.

Title: GDPR Delete Not Implemented
What's The Problem?: User requests deletion of their data. System has no way to delete them from everywhere (users table, applications, chat history).
What Happens To Users/Business?: GDPR violation = fines. Customer unhappy. Can't comply with legal request.
How To Fix It: Add delete user endpoint: Remove from users, applications, chat_sessions, token_usage tables. Record deletion reason.
What Gets Better After Fix: GDPR compliant. User data completely deleted. Legal requirement met.

Title: No Change Tracking UI
What's The Problem?: Changes are tracked (who changed what), but there's no UI for admins to see this history. Compliance guys have to query database manually.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Can't prove changes in audits. Compliance review is hard. Manual work.
How To Fix It: Build audit trail UI: Show list of all changes (created by, changed from, changed to, when). Filterable by date/user.
What Gets Better After Fix: Instant compliance answers. Audits easy. Professional system.

Title: .env Not Validated
What's The Problem?: Missing environment variables (.env values) are not checked on startup. Server starts anyway. Hours later, something breaks.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Confusing runtime errors appear hours later. Hard to debug. Production failure.
How To Fix It: On server startup, check if required env vars exist. Show clear error: 'Missing DATABASE_URL' before anything else.
What Gets Better After Fix: Fails fast with clear message. Easy to fix. No confusing runtime errors.

Title: No Graceful Degradation
What's The Problem?: If email server down, whole app assumes it's down. If WhatsApp fails, whole app fails. No fallback.
What Happens To Users/Business?: One service down = entire system down. Single point of failure.
How To Fix It: Try each service independently. If email down, continue without it. Log error. Retry later.
What Gets Better After Fix: One service down, others keep working. Resilient system. Better uptime.

Title: Type Mismatch (UUID vs TEXT)
What's The Problem?: In one table, bank_id is stored as UUID (special ID format). In another table, bank_id is TEXT (just letters and numbers). They don't match.
What Happens To Users/Business?: When you try to connect applications to banks, the system gets confused. You lose records. The database gets broken data.
How To Fix It: Make all bank_ids the same format (UUID everywhere, not mix and match). Update the database migration script.
What Gets Better After Fix: All tables match. Records don't get lost. Database works correctly. No orphaned records.

Title: Missing Important Columns
What's The Problem?: The applications table doesn't store important info: How many months for loan? What interest rate? How much EMI? What processing fee?
What Happens To Users/Business?: Banks ask for this info, but it's not in your system. You can't show customers their EMI. No proof of calculations. Reports fail.
How To Fix It: Add 8 new columns: tenure_months, interest_rate_offered, emi_calculated, processing_fee, etc. Update forms to fill these.
What Gets Better After Fix: Banks can get required info. Customers see their EMI. All calculations are recorded. Reports work.

Title: No Database Indexes
What's The Problem?: Right now, when you search for a user or application, the database checks EVERY record one by one. With 10,000 customers, this takes 450ms every time.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Admin dashboard is slow. When admin needs to find a lead, they wait 5+ seconds. App feels broken. Users get frustrated.
How To Fix It: Create 'shortcuts' in the database called indexes. Indexes make searches instant (5ms instead of 450ms). Like a book's table of contents.
What Gets Better After Fix: Searches become super fast. Admin can find records instantly. Dashboard feels responsive again.

Title: Hard Delete with No Recovery
What's The Problem?: Right now when you delete something, it's gone forever. No recovery. And there's no record of WHO deleted it or WHEN.
What Happens To Users/Business?: If you accidentally delete important data, it's lost. For compliance audits, you can't prove who changed what.
How To Fix It: Add 'soft delete': Don't actually remove data, just mark it as deleted. Add columns: deleted_at, deleted_by, deletion_reason.
What Gets Better After Fix: Can recover accidentally deleted data. Compliance team can prove who deleted what. No more 'oops, can't get it back'.

Title: Too Few Database Connections
What's The Problem?: Right now, the database can only handle 20 people at the same time. If 21 people try to use it, number 21 gets blocked.
What Happens To Users/Business?: When multiple admins use the system, it slows down. After 20 users, system fails. Doesn't scale.
How To Fix It: Increase connection pool from 20 to 50. Add timeout for long queries (30 seconds). Reconnect faster when idle.
What Gets Better After Fix: System allows 50+ users at same time. Doesn't crash. Handles real business growth without rebuilding.

Title: Can't Track Data Changes
What's The Problem?: The system records that a status was changed, but doesn't record WHAT changed. No old value, new value, or what field was touched.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Compliance asks 'Why was this application status changed from Pending to Rejected?' Answer: No idea. Total audit failure.
How To Fix It: Add columns to track: old_status, new_status, changed_fields. When anything changes, record it with timestamp and user.
What Gets Better After Fix: Can answer any compliance question. Complete audit trail. Know exactly what changed, when, and by who.

Title: Vague Error Messages
What's The Problem?: When something breaks, user sees: 'Error: Database error' or just 'Failed'. No detail about what's wrong.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User confused. Can't help support team troubleshoot. Can't fix their issue. Frustration.
How To Fix It: Show specific errors: 'Email already exists' or 'Phone must be 10 digits'. Tell user how to fix it.
What Gets Better After Fix: Users understand the problem. Can self-fix. Support doesn't get confused calls. Better support experience.

Title: No Logging System
What's The Problem?: When prod breaks, you have no logs to see what happened. Can't find the bug. Customers are angry. You're helpless.
What Happens To Users/Business?: App breaks and you have no idea why. Can't debug. Takes hours to figure out. Revenue lost.
How To Fix It: Add structured logging: Every request logged with timestamp, user id, endpoint, response time, errors.
What Gets Better After Fix: Know exactly what broke and when. Debug instantly. Fix problems in minutes, not hours.

Title: Crashes from Unhandled Errors
What's The Problem?: Async operations (background jobs) fail silently. If something goes wrong in background, server crashes without warning.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Server crashes randomly. Users lose connection. App becomes unreliable.
How To Fix It: Wrap async code with try-catch. Log all errors. Don't let one error crash the whole system.
What Gets Better After Fix: Server stays stable. Errors logged but don't crash. App stays reliable.

Title: No Email Fallback
What's The Problem?: If email server fails, the entire API request fails. Admin can't update records because email won't send.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Admins blocked from doing their job. Critical operations fail. Bottleneck.
How To Fix It: Make email optional: Try to send email. If fails, continue anyway. Log the error. Retry later.
What Gets Better After Fix: Admins can work even if email is down. No false failures. Operations continue.

Title: No Structured Logging
What's The Problem?: Logs are random text: sometimes has timestamp, sometimes doesn't. Hard to correlate logs across services. Can't search logs easily.
What Happens To Users/Business?: When debugging, logs are a mess. Can't find patterns. Hard to diagnose production issues.
How To Fix It: Use structured logging (JSON format): Every log has: timestamp, user_id, endpoint, status, response_time, error. queryable.
What Gets Better After Fix: Logs are searchable and organized. Easy debugging. Can trace requests end-to-end.

Title: No Performance Metrics
What's The Problem?: No visibility into what's slow. Is it the database? API? Chat? Where's the bottleneck? No data.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Can't optimize. Don't know where to focus. App gets slower over time without knowing why.
How To Fix It: Track metrics: Response time, queries per second, cache hit rate, token usage. Show in admin dashboard.
What Gets Better After Fix: See what's slow. Know exactly where to optimize. Data-driven decisions.

Title: Too Few DB Connections
What's The Problem?: Database pool set to max 20 connections. If 21 people request at same time, person #21 waits or fails.
What Happens To Users/Business?: After 20 concurrent users, app gets slow or breaks. Can't handle real load.
How To Fix It: Increase pool from 20 to 50. Add idle timeout 60s. Allow system to handle more people.
What Gets Better After Fix: Scales to 50+ users. More headroom. System doesn't break under normal load.

Title: Queries Never Timeout
What's The Problem?: If a query hangs or gets stuck, it hangs FOREVER. Blocks the whole connection. Server eventually runs out of connections.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Server becomes unresponsive. Everything slows down. Eventually crashes from no available connections.
How To Fix It: Add 30-second query timeout. Kill stuck queries automatically. Free up connections.
What Gets Better After Fix: No hung queries. Resources freed. System responsive. No cascade failures.

Title: Bank Data Fetched Every Time
What's The Problem?: Every time user loads the app, all bank data is fetched fresh from database. Already loaded before, no caching.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Unnecessary queries. Database load increases. API slower. Wasting resources.
How To Fix It: Cache bank data in memory. Refresh every 30 minutes or when admin updates. First load still fast.
What Gets Better After Fix: Reduces query load by 80%. App loads faster. Database less stressed.

Title: Chat Response Wait Time
What's The Problem?: When user asks AI a question, they wait 2-3 seconds for the FULL response. Nothing appears until complete.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User stares at blank screen. Feels stuck. Doesn't know if typing is happening. Bad UX.
How To Fix It: Stream response: Show text as it arrives (like ChatGPT). User sees 'thinking...' then chunks of text appearing.
What Gets Better After Fix: Feels instant. User sees thinking process. Much better perceived speed. Professional feel.

Title: Missing Authentication Checks
What's The Problem?: Right now, anyone can visit admin pages. There's no login check, so a random person could pretend to be an admin and change everything.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Hackers can access secret admin panels, steal data, delete records, change bank offers - total disaster.
How To Fix It: Add a login verification system. Check if user is logged in BEFORE showing admin pages. Use tokens or sessions to confirm identity.
What Gets Better After Fix: Admin pages become protected. Only real admins can access them. No strangers can break things.

Title: No Input Validation
What's The Problem?: When users type information (email, phone, amounts), the system never checks if it's real. Bad people can type weird code to break the database.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Users might lose data. Bad passwords work fine. Hackers can insert harmful code that deletes everything or steals information.
How To Fix It: Check every input field: Is email real? Is phone 10 digits? Is amount a number? Reject anything that looks wrong.
What Gets Better After Fix: System becomes safe. Only correct information goes into database. Hackers can't inject harmful code. Data stays clean.

Title: API Keys Exposed in Comments
What's The Problem?: Right now, secret API keys are written in the .env file in plain text and in code comments. If this code goes to GitHub, hackers see the keys.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Hackers get free access to use your Gemini AI service, run up huge bills, steal your credits, or break the API.
How To Fix It: Remove all API keys from comments. Move them to .env file only. Never write them in code. Use environment variables.
What Gets Better After Fix: Keys stay secret. Even if code is public, keys are hidden. Safe from hackers.

Title: No Rate Limiting
What's The Problem?: Right now, someone can spam the API 1000 times per second without being blocked. No protection against quick repeated attacks.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Hackers can try passwords 1000s of times per second. They can fill your database with fake data. App becomes slow for real users.
How To Fix It: Add a limit: 10 requests per minute per user. If someone goes over, block them for 5 minutes. Simple counter system.
What Gets Better After Fix: Hackers can't spam. Brute force attacks fail. App stays fast. Only normal users can use it.

Title: Sensitive Info in Comments
What's The Problem?: Passwords, email addresses, and other secrets are written as comments in the code so developers remember.
What Happens To Users/Business?: If code leaks, hackers see all the secret usernames and passwords written in comments.
How To Fix It: Delete all comments with passwords. Move sensitive info to .env. Use generic comments instead of real values.
What Gets Better After Fix: Code becomes safe to share. No secrets visible in comments. Passwords stay actually secret.

Title: Sign-In Tab Gets Stuck
What's The Problem?: When you click Sign-In tab, error messages appear. Then you click Sign-Up tab, and old error messages STILL SHOW.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Confusing. User thinks their info is wrong when they're on a different form. Not user-friendly.
How To Fix It: Clear error messages when user switches tabs. Use state to remember which tab they're on. Clean up old messages.
What Gets Better After Fix: Clean slate when switching. No confusing old errors. User experience becomes less frustrating.

Title: No Password Strength Meter
What's The Problem?: When user creates password, they don't know if it's strong or weak. No indicator, no requirements shown.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User creates weak password '123456' thinking it's safe. Account gets hacked. Users blame the system.
How To Fix It: Show password requirements and a meter: Red (weak) → Green (strong). Show: 8+ chars, 1 number, 1 special char required.
What Gets Better After Fix: Users create stronger passwords. Accounts are safer. Fewer hacked accounts. User education built-in.

Title: No Back Button
What's The Problem?: User clicks 'Forgot Password' by accident, now stuck on that page with no way to go back to login.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Frustration. User has to reload page or use browser back button. Bad UX.
How To Fix It: Add 'Back to Login' button on forgot password form. One click to return.
What Gets Better After Fix: Easy navigation. User not stuck. Smooth flow. User doesn't have to fiddle with browser.

Title: No Email Verification
What's The Problem?: User signs up with fake email. System accepts it. No verification email sent. User continues with invalid email.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Can't contact user. Notifications bounce. User can't reset password later. No real email on file.
How To Fix It: Send verification email: User clicks link to verify. Only then can they access app.
What Gets Better After Fix: Real emails only. Contactable users. Password resets work. Fewer fake accounts.

Title: Phone Doesn't Auto-Format
What's The Problem?: User types phone as 9876543210, but it doesn't show as 98765-43210 while typing. Looks messy.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User experience is not smooth. Looks like a bad app. Professional apps auto-format.
How To Fix It: As user types, auto-format: 9876543210 becomes 9876543210 → 9876-543210. Live formatting.
What Gets Better After Fix: Professional experience. Looks like a real app. User friction gone.

Title: Company Search Shows Only 5
What's The Problem?: User wants to find their company but only 5 results show. Their company is #6 or beyond. They can't find it.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User gives up. Bad experience. They leave the form. Funnel breaks.
How To Fix It: Show 5 results with a 'Show More' button or scroll in dropdown. Load all 500+ companies with search.
What Gets Better After Fix: User finds their company. Completes form. Better conversion. No frustration.

Title: No Submit Spinner
What's The Problem?: User clicks submit button. Page looks the same. User doesn't know if it's processing or if they need to click again.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User clicks multiple times. Form submits twice. Data duplicates. Confusion.
How To Fix It: Add loading spinner on button: 'Please wait...' or just a spinner icon. Disable retry.
What Gets Better After Fix: User knows it's processing. One submission only. No duplicates. Clear feedback.

Title: Admin Can't See All Leads
What's The Problem?: Admin can only see first 100 applications. Can't see application #101. No pagination buttons.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Admin thinks you only have 100 customers. Can't manage other leads. Broken business operations.
How To Fix It: Add pagination. Show 20 per page. Add Next/Previous buttons. Like turning pages.
What Gets Better After Fix: Admin sees all leads. Can manage full customer base. No data remains hidden.

Title: No Loan Amount Help
What's The Problem?: Form asks 'How much loan do you need?' User has no idea. No suggestion, no context about eligibility.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User guesses. Requests too little (loses opportunity) or too much (gets rejected instantly). Bad outcomes.
How To Fix It: Show recommendation based on income: '₹20K income → Eligible for ₹50K-100K'. Show risk level: Conservative/Moderate/Risky.
What Gets Better After Fix: User makes informed choice. Better loan requests. More approvals. Better outcomes.

Title: No Income Guidance
What's The Problem?: Form asks for monthly income but no context about impact. User doesn't understand how this affects eligibility.
What Happens To Users/Business?: User confused. Doesn't understand loan capacity. Makes bad decisions. Feels unsupported.
How To Fix It: Show: 'Your available capacity: ₹45K (Income ₹60K - EMIs ₹15K)'. Show eligibility impact.
What Gets Better After Fix: User understands consequences. Makes smart decisions. Feels supported. Better outcomes.

Title: No Email Checking
What's The Problem?: When user enters email, the system doesn't check if it's real. You can type 'notanemail123' and it accepts it.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Bad emails in database bounce. You can't contact users. User data is garbage. No real communication happens.
How To Fix It: Check email format with regex (simple formula). Check if @ and . exist. Suggest fixes for typos (gmail.com not gmial.com).
What Gets Better After Fix: Only real emails accepted. Messages reach customers. Communication works. No bad data.

Title: No Phone Validation
What's The Problem?: Users can enter phone as '123' or '12345678901' and it accepts anything. Indian phones must be 10 digits.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Phone numbers in database are wrong. Can't send WhatsApp. Notifications fail. Can't reach customers.
How To Fix It: Check phone format: Must be 10 digits. Must be numbers only. Auto-format as 98765-43210 while typing.
What Gets Better After Fix: Only valid Indian phones accepted. WhatsApp works. Notifications arrive. Contact customers reliably.

Title: No Age Limit
What's The Problem?: User can enter age as 200 or 5. System accepts 0 or 999 years old - nonsense values.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Bad data in database. Can't calculate loan eligibility correctly. Age-based restrictions fail. Business logic breaks.
How To Fix It: Add age validation: Only accept 18-70 years. Reject anything outside this range. Use slider for better UX.
What Gets Better After Fix: Only realistic ages accepted. Eligibility calculations work. Age cannot be exploited. Business logic stays correct.

Title: No Income Validation
What's The Problem?: User enters income as 0 or 999,999,999 and system accepts it. No minimum or maximum check.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Loan calculations based on fake income. Some users get huge loans (bad), others get nothing. System becomes unreliable.
How To Fix It: Add min/max check: Income must be between 10K and 500K. Reject values outside range. Use slider.
What Gets Better After Fix: Only realistic income accepted. Loan calculations accurate. No one game-plays the system.

Title: No CIBIL Score Validation
What's The Problem?: CIBIL score should be 300-900. System accepts 50, 999999, or any number. No validation.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Bad CIBIL data breaks eligibility. Banks reject because data looks fake. Credit decisions become wrong.
How To Fix It: Add validation: CIBIL must be 300-900. Offer buttons: Poor (300-600), Fair, Good, Excellent. No free entry of numbers.
What Gets Better After Fix: Only valid CIBIL scores accepted. Banks trust your data. Eligibility correctly evaluated. Fewer rejections.

Title: No File Size Limit
What's The Problem?: When uploading documents, there's no size check. Someone uploads a 1GB file and crashes the server.
What Happens To Users/Business?: Server memory overflows. System crashes. Everyone loses connection. Angry customers. Business down.
How To Fix It: Set max file size: 5MB per file, 50MB total. Check file size BEFORE uploading. Show error if too big.
What Gets Better After Fix: Can't crash server with huge files. System stays stable. All users protected from one person's bad upload.
